# Mood & Money Lab

> Research lab at University College Dublin investigating the relationship between financial behaviour and mental health.

## About

The Mood & Money Lab is based in the School of Computer Science at University College Dublin (UCD), Ireland. Our research explores how objective financial data — such as spending patterns and transaction history — can serve as passive indicators of mental health, with a focus on conditions such as bipolar disorder and depression. We combine human-centered AI, applied machine learning, and participatory research methods.

## People

- **Mark Matthews** (Principal Investigator) — mark.matthews@ucd.ie
- **Dara Adedeji** (PhD Candidate) — oluwadara.adedeji@ucdconnect.ie

## Research

Active and ongoing projects include:
- Passive assessment of mental health through financial behaviour
- Scoping review of mental health and financial variables
- Participatory study on money and mental health

## Publications

Peer-reviewed journal articles, conference papers, and preprints. Notable venues include CHI, EAI PervasiveHealth, and others in the HCI and digital health space.

## Contact

- Email: mark.matthews@ucd.ie
- Institution: School of Computer Science, University College Dublin, Belfield, Dublin 4, Ireland
- Website: https://moodmoney.ucd.ie
